page: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the DLSv5 family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores running at 2.8 GHz, with Hyper-Threading enabled. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This configuration does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a multi-core stress-ng score of 93251.87 and a Passmark CPU score of 76315.59. The Intel Xeon processor supports AVX-512 vector extensions and cryptographic acceleration flags such as AES and SHA_NI, which contribute to a top-tier Passmark encryption score of 87240.76. Memory performance is also strong, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 329.31 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 140.95 GB/sec. Single-core performance remains average across stress-ng and Geekbench tests.

The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this server as a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The lack of local storage requires external disk attachment, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider. Given its strong multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and high static web serving throughput, the server is well-suited for batch processing, CI/CD pipelines, web serving, and multi-threaded computational tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is a compute-optimized x86_64 server from the DLSv5 family, featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs across 48 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 192.0 GB of RAM, representing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and contains no local storage or GPUs. The server delivers strong multi-core performance, supported by AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration, as evidenced by its high Passmark CPU and encryption benchmark scores. Uncached memory read bandwidth reaches 329.31 GB/sec, and multi-threaded decompression performance is strong. This server is highly efficient for compute-intensive workloads that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios, making it suitable for software compilation, batch processing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is a compute-optimized server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is a compute-optimized server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-core-count compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and a balanced two-gigabyte-per-vCPU memory configuration.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8370C processors at 2.8 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Strong multi-core performance and top-tier cryptographic acceleration
No bundled local storage, requiring external Premium Storage volumes
Optimized for software compilation, batch processing, and web serving
